meta.title: Forecast Wing — pre-registered forecasts
meta.context: The museum's macro wing: 14 binary forecasts on Modern Mercantilism, AI, and their intersection — the same pre-registration discipline the museum applies to trading strategies, pointed at the macro decade. Objective resolution criteria; status tracked by the nightly pipeline.
meta.probabilities_note: Priced by the curator 2026-07-21; open to dated revision on incoming data through 2026-07-25, frozen thereafter. Revision 2026-07-23a: igv 29->35, btos 45->52 (staged weekly evidence). Revision 2026-07-23b (adversarial pass): wedge 33->37 (pharma Sec-232 at 100% starts 2026-07-31, lifting the statutory base), nvda 39->34 (two US approvals, zero deliveries — the demand-side veto is the binding one), igv 35->30 (index self-hedge: the disruptors sit inside IGV; tier to book), btos 52->58 (post-rewording trend arithmetic). acm holds 55 as a declared fade against the repricing consensus — both institutional views cited in the appendix. Revision 2026-07-23c: usg-equity 45->52, tier to book (direction-vs-ownership distinction argued in the framework session: the state prefers floors/offtake/licenses, but the stake pipeline runs). Tariff pair holds for the 2026-07-24 Section 122 expiry / Section 301 final action. engine_band is a base-rate input computed before pricing, shown for transparency.
meta.calibration_record: Jump Trading World Cup Probability Cup 2026: top 6% of entrants, 620 forecasts, mean Brier 0.231 (coin-flip = 0.25).
meta.license: CC BY 4.0 — cite The Risk Museum
meta.tiers_note: Each forecast carries a declared conviction tier: CONVICTION (defended under pressure), AGAINST THE CROWD (a priced disagreement with consensus), DECLARED UNCERTAINTY (honestly near the coin, and saying so), or WORKING BOOK. Tiers follow the probabilities; if a price is revised before the freeze, its tier moves with it.

forecasts.title: The Section 301 rebuild
forecasts.statement: On 2026-12-31, Section 301 tariffs of >=10% ad valorem from USTR's 2026 forced-labor investigations are in legal effect on imports from >=20 of the 60 investigated economies (EU counts as one).
forecasts.resolution_criteria: Resolves YES if, as of 11:59pm ET 2026-12-31, final action(s) under Section 301 (19 U.S.C. 2411-2417) in USTR's forced-labor proceeding (60 investigations initiated 2026-03-12; proposed 10%/12.5% duties per FR Doc. 2026-11296, docket USTR-2026-0265) have imposed an additional ad valorem duty of at least 10% then in legal effect on imports from at least 20 distinct economies. Counting rules: economies as USTR enumerates them, EU = one; an economy counts only if a >=10% duty under THIS proceeding was imposed by Federal Register final-action notice, has reached its effective date, is not suspended/terminated/reduced below 10% (suspended duties do NOT count), and is not vacated/stayed/universally enjoined by a court order then in effect (plaintiff-specific relief does not disqualify). The 10% threshold is this proceeding's rate alone, not stacked with other authorities. Partial product coverage qualifies. Duties under Sections 122/232/338, IEEPA or successors, or other 301 dockets do not count. Fewer than 20 qualifying economies at the moment = NO. Resolve from the Federal Register, USTR releases, and CIT/CAFC/SCOTUS dockets.
forecasts.base_rate_current: SCOTUS voided the IEEPA tariffs 2026-02-20; the Section 122 stopgap expires 2026-07-24; 60 investigations opened 2026-03-12, final action pending after the 2026-07-07 hearing. The 2018-19 China 301 lists went proposal-to-effect in ~2-4 months.
forecasts.bw_claim: Modern Mercantilism is instrument-agnostic: the regime rebuilds its tariff floor through Section 301 after the IEEPA defeat, or institutions bind.

forecasts.title: Statutory vs collected — the wedge
forecasts.statement: US calculated duties as a share of customs value of goods imports for consumption exceed 9.0% for calendar year 2027.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: Total calculated duties divided by total customs value, imports for consumption, all countries and commodities, CY2027, from USITC DataWeb (Census source data). Vintage: the annual figure as retrievable after the December-2027 statistical month first publishes (~early Feb 2028) and on or before 2028-03-31; later revisions ignored. Unrounded ratio; YES iff strictly >9.0% (exactly 9.0% = NO). This is the calculated-duties measure (PWBM tracker basis), NOT Treasury/CBP cash collections — refunds do not enter. If DataWeb is unavailable: official Census imports-for-consumption data with calculated-duty and customs-value fields (USA Trade Online); never collections data. If December 2027 is unpublished by 2028-03-31: the same ratio over cumulative CY2027 year-to-date months available.
forecasts.base_rate_current: Yale statutory rate ~11.8% pre-substitution vs 7.2% collected (May 2026, PWBM) — a 3-4.6pp wedge from exemptions, substitution, and transshipment. Pre-2025 baseline was 2.3%.
forecasts.bw_claim: Tariffs as a durable revenue and rebalancing instrument — tested at the cash register, not the announcement.

forecasts.title: Magnet flows — the chokepoint in a customs line
forecasts.statement: China's CY2027 exports of metal permanent magnets (HS 8505.11, containing sintered NdFeB) to the US exceed its 2022-2024 annual average tonnage in GACC data.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: Primary: China GACC (stats.customs.gov.cn), exports to the US under HS 8505.11 (sum of all Chinese 8-digit children incl. 8505.1110), kg converted to tonnes. YES if the CY2027 total (first GACC release covering full-year 2027) strictly exceeds the mean of CY2022/2023/2024 annual totals as displayed on the same platform at resolution; equal or lower = NO. First-retrieved figures govern. Fallback: if GACC has not published CY2027 country-level 8505.11 by 2028-02-21, or has discontinued/reclassified the line for 2022-2027, use US Census general imports from China, HS 8505.11 (USA Trade Online, customs quantity), identical comparison within the Census series. Resolution date 2028-02-28.
forecasts.base_rate_current: H1-2026 flows still ~20% below the 2022-24 average despite the truce; extraterritorial controls postponed only one year; the reciprocal-tariff suspension expires 2026-11-10; US demand substitution is ramping.
forecasts.bw_claim: Weaponized interdependence — chokepoints as standing instruments of statecraft. The purest Mercantilism x AI collision visible in a single customs line.

forecasts.title: The double-veto chip trade
forecasts.statement: Nvidia reports >=$2B of Data Center revenue attributable to China (incl. Hong Kong) for any single fiscal quarter from FY2027 Q2 through FY2028 Q4.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: Covered quarters: fiscal quarters ending after 2026-07-21 and on or before 2028-01-31 (7 quarters); earlier quarters (incl. the $4.6B H20 quarter) do NOT count. YES if any official Nvidia disclosure published by 2028-03-31 (10-Q/10-K, 8-K or press release, CFO commentary, or executive earnings-call statement per the posted transcript) states China Data Center (or DC compute) revenue >=$2.0B for a single covered quarter. Nvidia's own attribution governs; 'approximately $2B' counts; exactly $2.0B = YES. Percentage disclosures count only if the lowest stated percentage times that quarter's DC revenue >=$2.0B; qualitative descriptors never trigger. Billing-location geography tables do NOT count (must be identified as DC revenue). Multi-quarter totals count only if >= N x $2.0B. Vintage: the latest Nvidia statement as of 2028-03-31; a restatement below $2.0B before then defeats it. NO if no qualifying disclosure by 2028-03-31.
forecasts.resolution_source: Nvidia SEC filings, releases, earnings transcripts
forecasts.base_rate_current: $4.6B/quarter pre-ban went to zero; two US approvals (H20 with 15% revenue share, H200 with 25% surcharge) produced zero deliveries — Beijing buy-local directives block purchases; B30A unapproved; guidance assumes no China DC revenue.
forecasts.bw_claim: Is US-China tech trade a negotiable toll regime or a structural mutual embargo? Both capitals now hold a veto — the uncrowded read.

forecasts.title: Do export controls work, or backfire?
forecasts.statement: China's semiconductor self-sufficiency (value of ICs fabricated in China-located fabs, any ownership, over value of China IC consumption) is reported at >=50% for CY2029 by TrendForce, TechInsights, or SEMI.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: Metric: IC-Insights-style 'China-based production' share of the China IC market, CY2029. Only whole-IC wafer-fab output qualifies — equipment, materials, wafers, EDA, AI-chips-only, node-class-only, or capacity-share figures do NOT. Source precedence: (1) TrendForce analyst research (not '[News]' relay posts); (2) TechInsights; (3) SEMI. Chinese-government targets or claims never qualify, even if republished. Take the highest-precedence source with a qualifying CY2029 estimate published by 2030-12-31, most recent estimate as of that date (latest revision governs). YES if >=50.0% as printed. If the source reports only the narrower Chinese-headquartered-only share: >=50% is still YES; <50% on that narrower share = NO. No qualifying estimate by 2030-12-31 = NO.
forecasts.base_rate_current: ~16% (2024) to ~28% (Q4 2025) on one definition set; equipment localization 25%→35%; Beijing's target is 80% by 2030. Constraints: domestic HBM caps accelerator output; SMIC advanced-node yields ~20%; no domestic EUV confirmed.
forecasts.bw_claim: The definitive test of whether containment preserves the US lead or accelerates Chinese self-sufficiency.

forecasts.title: The +140bp claim, tested in the national accounts
forecasts.statement: The combined contribution of Information Processing Equipment plus Software to US real GDP growth for CY2026 (annual-average basis, BEA NIPA Table 1.5.2) is >=1.00 percentage points.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: Sum the published annual-2026 contribution lines for 'Information processing equipment' (parent line under Equipment) and 'Software' (under Intellectual Property Products) in BEA NIPA Table 1.5.2. Annual-frequency figure (2026 average over 2025 average), not Q4/Q4. Governing vintage: the Q4-2026 third estimate (late March 2027) if published by 2027-03-31; else the most recent BEA release with annual-2026 data by that date; else the first release ever containing it. Later revisions ignored. Published two-decimal values summed, no re-rounding; exactly 1.00 = YES, <=0.99 = NO. Renamed lines resolve to successor lines; a renumbered table resolves to the successor expanded-detail contributions table.
forecasts.base_rate_current: A widely-circulated institutional estimate (Jan 2026): AI capex contributes ~+140bp to 2026 growth. The observable equipment+software lines contributed ~0.8-1.0pp in 2025 vs a ~0.3-0.5pp pre-2023 decade norm. >=1.00pp is dot-com-peak territory.
forecasts.bw_claim: DIRECT test of the most-cited institutional claim of the cycle — AI capex contributing >=140bp to 2026 US growth — in the closest public series.

forecasts.title: The first falsifiable bust signature
forecasts.statement: Combined CY2027 capital expenditure of Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, and Meta exceeds combined CY2026 capex by >=20%.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: Per-company capex = the capital-expenditure line in the investing section of the cash-flow statement (10-Q/10-K): MSFT 'Additions to property and equipment'; GOOGL and META 'Purchases of property and equipment'; AMZN 'Purchases of property and equipment' GROSS (proceeds not netted). Finance-lease principal excluded for all four. GOOGL/AMZN/META: fiscal-year 10-K figure. MSFT calendar-year: FY 10-K annual minus 6-month YTD ended prior Dec 31 (Q2 10-Q) plus 6-month YTD ended that Dec 31 (next Q2 10-Q). All figures as first reported; restatements ignored. YES if the CY2027 sum >= 1.20 x the CY2026 sum (tie = YES), in USD millions. Fallbacks: missing 10-K/10-Q by 2028-02-10 → 8-K earnings-release cash-flow figure; still missing by 2028-03-15 → exclude that company from BOTH years. Acquired or stopped filing → successor's figure or exclude from both. Hard deadline 2028-03-15.
forecasts.base_rate_current: CY2025 combined ~$380-400B; CY2026 guidance ~$500B+; the series has never declined year-over-year since 2015; the telecom analog flipped to -40% in year five (2001).
forecasts.bw_claim: Compute +60%/yr and capex-boom continuation; a miss below +20% is the first falsifiable signature of the bust chapter the consensus macro view warns about.

forecasts.title: Is scarcity pricing structural?
forecasts.statement: PJM's 2029/2030 Base Residual Auction (closing 2026-12-15) clears RTO-wide at its price cap, or at/above $325.00/MW-day — a fourth consecutive at-cap outcome.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: YES if PJM's official 2029/30 BRA results (report or news release on pjm.com) show an RTO-wide clearing price that (a) equals the maximum allowable price for that auction — the FERC-approved collar cap ($175-$325 through 2029/30, annually adjusted) or successor VRR cap — by numeric match to the cent, OR PJM stating it cleared at the cap; or (b) is >=$325.00/MW-day (nominal, UCAP, as reported; tie counts) — a backstop if the collar is vacated or redefined. The latest official PJM figures as of the resolution check (on or before 2027-12-31) govern. No official results by 2027-12-31 → NO.
forecasts.base_rate_current: Cap hit three-for-three (2026/27 $329.17; 2027/28 $333.44; 2028/29 $325.00). Supply cannot respond by 2029/30: interconnection request-to-COD >4 years, turbine lead times 6-8 years, major turbine vendors sold out through 2030; datacenter demand growing ~5%/yr in commercial sales.
forecasts.bw_claim: AI capex as a durable macro force — tested at its most binding physical constraint (power), not at the spending line.

forecasts.title: Reserve managers' revealed preference
forecasts.statement: Central banks' net gold purchases total >=750 tonnes for CY2026, per the World Gold Council's Gold Demand Trends FY2026 report.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: YES if the WGC's FY2026 Gold Demand Trends report (expected late Jan/early Feb 2027, gold.org) states full-year 2026 net central-bank demand (the 'Central banks and other institutions' line) >=750.0 tonnes. The table figure at stated precision governs (749.8 = NO even if the narrative rounds to 750); a whole-tonne figure is used as stated. First-published FY2026 figure; revisions ignored. Exactly 750.0 = YES. Fallback: no WGC figure by 2027-03-31 → the published Metals Focus FY2026 net official-sector figure, same threshold; neither by 2027-03-31 → NO.
forecasts.resolution_source: WGC Gold Demand Trends (fallback: Metals Focus)
forecasts.base_rate_current: 2022: 1,136t; 2024: ~1,092t; 2025: 863t (fourth-largest year ever); WGC's own 2026 projection is 750-850t; gold ~$4,054/oz after +65% in 2025 — a price headwind to tonnage. The 2010-21 average was 473t.
forecasts.bw_claim: DIRECT: gold has displaced Treasuries as the #2 (or #1 at market value, per ECB) reserve asset; a fourth straight 750t+ year at record prices makes the sanctions-era shift permanent.

forecasts.title: Level vs share — the contrarian read
forecasts.statement: Total foreign holdings of US Treasuries (TIC Major Foreign Holders 'Grand Total', first print) reach >=$10 trillion for any data month from July 2026 through June 2028.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: YES if the Grand Total of the Treasury's mfh.txt table is >= $10,000.0B as published to one decimal for any data month July 2026 through June 2028, using the FIRST publication of that month (a new month appears in the release ~mid month+2). Revisions and benchmark restatements ignored. mfh.txt is overwritten in place — first prints are verified contemporaneously or via a release-date archive (e.g. Wayback); the archived first print governs. A renamed table resolves to the successor total-foreign-UST line. The June-2028 print is scheduled ~2028-08-17. NO if no qualifying first print by 2028-08-31; publication delays do not extend the window; later-published figures do not count.
forecasts.base_rate_current: ~$9.2T (Dec 2025), +$1.5T over four years (~+$375B/yr) even as China exits (~-$0.7T) and the foreign share of federal debt falls; May-2026 first print ~$9,370B; record $9,490B (Feb 2026). Trend crosses $10T ~mid-2028 — window-edge by design.
forecasts.bw_claim: The flow side of 'foreign demand for US debt is structurally weakening' — tested in the level series the de-dollarization debate ignores in favor of share data.

forecasts.title: Where the collision prices
forecasts.statement: The NY Fed's ACM 10-year term premium (ACMTP10) prints >=1.00% on five consecutive trading days at some point between 2027-01-01 and 2028-06-30.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: YES if the published daily ACMTP10 series shows unrounded values >=1.00 (percent) on 5 consecutive observations dated within the window (endpoints inclusive); weekends/holidays/data gaps do not break a run. Vintage handling (the series is re-estimated): resolves YES permanently the first time any then-current vintage, checked at least monthly during the window, shows a qualifying streak; re-estimation cannot reverse a YES. If no check found a streak, NO per the first vintage published after 2028-06-30. If discontinued: Kim-Wright 10y (FRED: THREEFYTP10), same threshold and rules from the discontinuation date. Both gone with no successor → NO.
forecasts.resolution_source: NY Fed ACM daily series (fallback: Kim-Wright)
forecasts.base_rate_current: ~0.47-0.73% (Jun-Jul 2026) — the first sustained positive stretch since 2023; 30y yield >5.1%; ~$10T of Treasuries rolling over in 2026; deficits ~5.8% of GDP; pre-2014 the series regularly sat above 1.5%.
forecasts.bw_claim: DIRECT: Dalio's 'How Countries Go Broke' — Treasury supply exceeding structural demand forces term-premium repricing; AI-capex deficits and mercantilist capital repatriation meet in this number.

forecasts.title: State capitalism: pipeline or one-offs?
forecasts.statement: The US federal government announces AND closes equity interests (common/preferred, warrants, or golden-share rights) in >=5 additional US-listed companies between 2026-07-01 and 2028-12-31, in semiconductors, critical minerals, batteries, or AI-datacenter infrastructure.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: Counts distinct US-listed issuers meeting ALL of: (1) NYSE/Nasdaq/national-exchange listed at initial announcement (OTC excluded; a stake in a private subsidiary counts toward its listed parent; one issuer counts once). (2) Sector per the agency announcement or the issuer's latest 10-K/20-F primary business: semiconductor design/manufacturing/equipment/materials; critical minerals (USGS list, incl. rare earths) mining/processing/refining/recycling/magnets; battery cells/materials/storage; or data centers, AI compute/cloud, or power contracted primarily to datacenters. Quantum, defense primes, and shipbuilding do not qualify. (3) A negotiated direct acquisition (from the company, or as a condition of federal support) of equity/warrants/convertibles/golden-share held by the USG, a federal agency, or a wholly-owned federal entity (incl. any sovereign wealth fund). Excludes: Federal Reserve actions; loan/grant/offtake-only support; CHIPS awards without equity; passive pension/insurance holdings; bankruptcy/litigation/tax/forfeiture equity; independent intermediaries. (4) FIRST public announcement of the specific deal on/after 2026-07-01 AND closing by 2028-12-31; deals first announced on or before 2026-06-30 are excluded even if they close later (excludes Intel, MP Materials, Lithium Americas, US Steel, Trilogy, USA Rare Earth, etc.). (5) Evidence: an official White House/agency release AND company SEC confirmation (8-K preferred). YES immediately upon the 5th distinct qualifying closing; otherwise NO after 2028-12-31.
forecasts.resolution_source: Agency releases cross-checked vs SEC EDGAR 8-Ks
forecasts.base_rate_current: ~4-5 chokepoint stakes in 2025 alone vs a near-zero peacetime base; ~30 direct-ownership deals / ~$26.7B since Jan 2025 per CFR's tracker; a $5B DFC equity revolver under the FY2026 NDAA.
forecasts.bw_claim: Every vulnerability gets weaponized, and the state directs private capital: five more closings = a program with a pipeline, not personality-driven one-offs.

forecasts.title: Buildout vs adoption, measured
forecasts.statement: The share of US businesses reporting current AI use in the Census Bureau's BTOS survey reaches >=25% in any biweekly period ending by 2028-12-31.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: YES if any BTOS biweekly period ending on or before 2028-12-31 shows a national weighted estimate >=25.0% answering Yes on the current-AI-use item in effect for that period (through Oct 2025: use 'in producing goods or services'; from Nov 2025: use 'in any of its business functions' in the two-week reference period), in any vintage of the public files at census.gov/hfp/btos published by 2029-01-31. The published one-decimal national estimate governs; exactly 25.0 = YES. Current use only, not the 'next six months' item. If reworded again → the single current-use item governs; if multiple current-use items → the broadest any-function item. NO if nothing qualifying is published by 2029-01-31 (incl. survey discontinuation or suspension).
forecasts.base_rate_current: ~3.9% (fall 2023) → ~9% (2025, old wording); the Nov-2025 rewording lifted the level to ~18% at end-2025; 19.8% at the period ending 2026-05-03 — the gap to 25% is ~5pp on the governing series.
forecasts.bw_claim: The buildout-vs-adoption pricing gap: YES = adoption arriving fast enough to justify the capex; NO = the gap persists into 2029 — the bubble-scenario precondition.

forecasts.title: The 82% survival bet
forecasts.statement: The iShares software ETF (IGV) underperforms SPY by >=25 percentage points in cumulative NAV total return from 2026-12-31 to 2029-12-31.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: YES if IGV cumulative NAV total return minus SPY cumulative NAV total return over the window is <= -25.0pp (exactly -25.0 = YES). Primary: each issuer's published 3-year average annual NAV total return as of 2029-12-31 (ishares.com / ssga.com), cumulative = ((1+r)^3 - 1) x 100, difference unrounded. If either figure is not published by 2030-01-31: chain issuer daily NAV with distributions reinvested on ex-date (Morningstar NAV TR is an acceptable equivalent). First figures published after 2029-12-31 govern. Fund closure/merger/delisting → chain-link the realized fund return with the total-return benchmark index (S&P North American Expanded Technology Software TR / S&P 500 TR or successors) through 2029-12-31.
forecasts.resolution_source: iShares/SSGA NAV total-return data; S&P DJ Indices fallback
forecasts.base_rate_current: A -25pp three-year sector shortfall vs the S&P is roughly a 1-in-5 event given 10-15%/yr tracking error; SaaS multiples already de-rated through 2025-26.
forecasts.bw_claim: DIRECT: the ~82% implied 10-year software survival probability — pricing whether AI is sustaining innovation for incumbents or creative destruction.
